data.title: Phoenix Education Partners Shares Surge in NYSE Debut
BXP, Inc. Announces Closing of $465 Million Financing for the Hub on Causeway
data.description:
BXP, Inc. announced the closing of a $465.0 million, 5.5-year, non-recourse mortgage loan secured by the podium and office tower at The Hub on Causeway in Boston, Massachusetts. The loan is scheduled to mature on April 9, 2031, and bears interest at a fixed rate of approximately 5.733% per annum. Proceeds from the loan were used to repay two existing loans on The Hub on Causeway having an aggregate outstanding principal balance of approximately $490 million.

This transaction represents BXP?s first ?Green Bond? mortgage financing, underscoring the quality of The Hub as a premier, sustainable workplace and highlighting BXP?s ongoing commitment to environmentally responsible development. The Hub on Causeway is a transformative transit-oriented ecosystem featuring an office tower that is home to creative and technology leaders, retail and entertainment spaces, a boutique hotel, a luxury residential tower, and a direct entrance to TD Garden and North Station.

The financing was led by Wells Fargo Bank, N.A., Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A., and Bank of America.

data.title: Trump says shutdown will lead to permanent cuts to Democratic programs
Nomura Sees Thailand's Central Bank Cutting Rates by 25bps in December 2025 and Q1 2026 to Reach 1.00% Terminal Rate
First Brands Is Great Company With Bad Balance Sheet, Says Marathon's Richards
data.description:
Marathon Asset Management LP Chief Executive Officer Bruce Richards says First Brands is a "great company" with a bad balance sheet. Marathon, a distressed-debt investor, bought the term loan of First Brands at around 40 cents on the dollar. "We feel good about that position because we think it's a great company that needs to be restructured," Richards said on "Bloomberg Open Interest."

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires

data.title: CDB Aviation Completes Mandate to Deliver Two A320neos to Azerbaijan Airlines
data.description:
CDB Aviation, a wholly owned Irish subsidiary of China Development Bank Financial Leasing Co., Ltd. (“CDB Leasing”), announced today the completion of a delivery mandate for two brand-new Airbus A320neo aircraft to Azerbaijan’s national air carrier, Azerbaijan Airlines (“AZAL”).

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20251009552033/en/

AZAL took delivery of the second, final A320neo on October 9, 2025, in Blagnac, France.

The announcement of the lease agreements in June 2024 marked CDB Aviation’s first transaction in Azerbaijan, which became the lessor’s newest customer within the region. The A320neos, powered by the CFM LEAP-1A26 engines, were introduced to AZAL’s fleet as part of the airline’s fleet modernization initiative, which will replace its older A320 and A319s with the new-generation versions.

“I would like to thank the AZAL and CDB Aviation teams for their excellent collaboration to date. We are confident that the addition of these latest generation A320neo Family aircraft will further boost AZAL’s sustainable growth,” commented Jie Chen, Chief Executive Officer of CDB Aviation.

“We are pleased to deepen our cooperation with CDB Aviation through the delivery of these new A320neo aircraft. The addition of these modern, fuel-efficient aircraft supports AZAL’s ongoing fleet renewal strategy and reflects our commitment to offering passengers a more comfortable and sustainable travel experience,” said Samir Rzayev, President of Azerbaijan Airlines.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release contains certain forward-looking statements, beliefs or opinions, including with respect to CDB Aviation’s business, financial condition, results of operations or plans. CDB Aviation cautions readers that no forward-looking statement is a guarantee of future performance and that actual results or other financial condition or performance measures could differ materially from those contained in the forward-looking statements. These forward-looking statements can be identified by the fact that they do not relate only to historical or current facts. Forward-looking statements sometimes use words such as ”may,” “will,” “seek,” “continue,” “aim,” “anticipate,” “target,” “projected,” “expect,” “estimate,” “intend,” “plan,” “goal,” “believe,” “achieve” or other terminology or words of similar meaning. These statements are based on the current beliefs and expectations of CDB Aviation’s management and are subject to significant risks and uncertainties. Actual results and outcomes may differ materially from those expressed in the forward-looking statements. Accordingly, you should not rely upon forward-looking statements as a prediction of actual results and we do not assume any responsibility for the accuracy or completeness of any of these forward-looking statements. Except as required by applicable law, we do not undertake any obligation to, and will not, update any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.

About Azerbaijan Airlines (“AZAL”)

AZAL holds the prestigious 4-Star Skytrax rating and has repeatedly been named Best Regional Airline in Central Asia and the CIS at the Skytrax World Airline Awards. As an IATA member, the airline continues to expand its network, drive digital innovation, and enhance passenger comfort. With a modern fleet that meets ICAO standards and an expanding route network, AZAL provides top-level service while strengthening Azerbaijan’s position in the global aviation market. Sustainable development is a cornerstone of AZAL’s strategy.

The airline is a signatory of the UN Global Compact, publishes its ESG commitments, and participates in IATA’s CO₂ Connect and IEnvA programs, reinforcing its position as a reliable and responsible partner in the international aviation industry. www.azal.az/en

About CDB Aviation

CDB Aviation is a wholly owned Irish subsidiary of China Development Bank Financial Leasing Co., Ltd. (“CDB Leasing”) a 40-year-old Chinese leasing company that is backed mainly by the China Development Bank. CDB Aviation is rated Investment Grade by Moody’s (A2), S&P Global (A), and Fitch (A+). China Development Bank is under the direct jurisdiction of the State Council of China and is one of the world’s largest development finance institution. It is also the largest Chinese bank for foreign investment and financing cooperation, long-term lending and bond issuance, enjoying Chinese sovereign credit rating.

CDB Leasing is the only leasing arm of the China Development Bank and a leading company in China’s leasing industry that has been engaged in aircraft, infrastructure, ship, commercial vehicle and construction machinery leasing and enjoys a Chinese sovereign credit rating. It took an important step in July 2016 to globalize and marketize its business – listing on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange (HKEX STOCK CODE: 1606). www.CDBAviation.aero

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20251009552033/en/

data.title: AstraZeneca breaks ground at $4.5 billion Virginia plant as drugmakers court Trump
data.description:
LONDON (Reuters) -AstraZeneca broke ground on a new plant in Virginia on Thursday and said it would spend $4.5 billion on the facility as drugmakers look to respond to President Donald Trump's call for more medicines to be made in the U.S. and at lower costs.

The site in Albemarle County, about 120 miles (193 km) southwest of Washington, will be the Anglo-Swedish company's largest manufacturing facility worldwide.

The investment is part of AstraZeneca's plan announced in July to spend $50 billion to expand U.S. research and manufacturing by 2030. AstraZeneca said on Thursday the plant will create 600 highly skilled jobs, and 3,000 more will be created for its construction. 

DRUGMAKERS LOOK TO RAMP UP U.S. MANUFACTURING

The company also said it was expanding plans for the site to include production of blockbuster cancer medicines along with future weight-loss and metabolic drugs. It said it would spend $500 million more than initially planned.

The groundbreaking was attended by a senior Trump administration health official, Dr Mehmet Oz, Administrator of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services.

"I congratulate AstraZeneca for their investment and invite other foreign manufacturers to follow suit," he said.

ANALYSTS ASK: ARE INDUSTRY MOVES LARGELY POLITICAL?

Trump has, using the threat of imposing tariffs on pharmaceutical imports, extracted a range of concessions from the industry. 

Last week U.S. drugmaker Pfizer announced at the White House that it would slash drug prices in Medicaid for low-income people and for new drugs in exchange for relief on tariffs, setting a tone for drugmaker peers to match.

Analysts say such moves may have little impact on overall revenues and that the impact on prices will also be limited, raising questions over whether they are largely political.

ASTRAZENECA CEO SORIOT NAVIGATES TRUMP'S DEMANDS ON INDUSTRY

AstraZeneca CEO Pascal Soriot, who earlier this year described his firm as a "very American company", has looked to balance Trump's demands on the sector. Last month, the company announced a full listing of its shares on the New York Stock Exchange and said it would soon sell diabetes and asthma drugs direct to U.S. patients at discounts of up to 70%.

On Thursday, Soriot said the Virginia facility would "strengthen America's national security and health sovereignty", after earlier this year criticising European governments for not spending more on medicines.

(Reporting by Maggie FickEditing by Adam Jourdan and Nia Williams)

By Maggie Fick

Toronto Stocks Ease Back on Recent Strength
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart


Shares on Canada's main exchange continued to pull back on recent gains Thursday.

In midday trading, the Toronto Stock Exchange's S&P/TSX Composite Index was 0.5% lower at 30334.37. That has it on track for a third straight daily decline after finishing at a fresh all-time high Monday.

Losses were spread widely across sectors, led by finance stocks as well as miners, as the recent surge in the price of gold eased. Technology-services stocks bucked the decline, buoyed by a rise in shares of index heavyweight Constellation Software.

The blue-chip S&P/TSX 60 was down 0.4% at 1784.88.

Among finance names, Fairfax Financial was down 1%, Manulife Financial was 1.1% weaker and Sun Life Financial declined 0.9%.

Big gold producers Barrick Mining and Agnico Eagle each lost more than 2% and B2Gold dropped 4%.


Other market movers:

Bausch Health was up 1.4% at C$9 after it said its shareholders approved the adoption of a poison-pill plan aimed at deterring any hostile or unsolicited takeover bid.

Richelieu Hardware's shares jumped 5.2% to C$34.58 after it reported a rise in third-quarter earnings, bolstered by a string of acquisitions.


Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-09-25 1223ET

Silver Hits Historic $50 Mark as Investors Rush -3-
data.description:
0212 GMT - China's gold miners stand to benefit from higher gold prices, HSBC Global Investment Research analysts say in a report. Gold prices could continue rising in the short term and into 1H 2026 due to factors such as geopolitical risks and financial-market turbulence, the analysts say. HSBC raises its average gold forecasts to $3,355/oz from $3,215/oz for 2025, to $3,950/oz from $3,125/oz for 2026 and to $3,600/oz from $2,925/oz for 2027. HSBC also raises target prices of Zijin Mining's H shares to HK$41.80 from HK$37.70, Shandong Gold Mining's A shares to CNY50.00 from CNY36.00 and Zhaojin Mining Industry's H shares to HK$37.10 from HK$23.70. It has buy ratings on all three stocks. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)


Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-09-25 1221ET

data.title: Silver Hits Historic $50 Mark as Investors Rush -2-
data.description:
1115 GMT - Gold prices remain slightly pressured by easing geopolitical risks in the Middle East and some profit-taking, with futures in New York down 0.3% to $4,059 a troy ounce. "Strong ETF inflows, momentum, and FOMO [fear of missing out]-driven buying have pushed prices into technically overbought territory, prompting some short-term caution," analysts at Saxo Bank say. Still, the precious metal remains well supported by a rush to safe-haven assets due to growing political uncertainties, prospects of further U.S. interest rate cuts and central-bank buying. Meanwhile, silver futures trade 0.9% lower to $48.55 an ounce. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

Oil Falls With Gaza Deal, U.S. Crude Stocks in Focus -- Market Talk

1053 GMT - Oil prices fall in afternoon trade, pressured by easing geopolitical risks in the Middle East and higher U.S. crude inventories. Brent slips 0.5% to $65.89 a barrel, while WTI is down 0.6% to $61.67 a barrel. Israel and Hamas agreed to the first phase of President Trump's peace plan for Gaza, a move that could reduce the region's risk premium. Meanwhile, U.S. crude inventories rose for a second straight week, sending bearish signals about demand in the world's largest oil consumer. Oil prices have fallen around 10% this year, as fears of an impending supply glut continue to weigh on sentiment. Citi analysts say sentiment remains "univocally bearish," with some doubting that a $60-a-barrel Brent floor will be enough to keep the market in balance. Others expect a more moderate price correction, with rising oil inventories outside key hubs expected to prevent sharp price drops. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

Palm Oil Gains on Strength in Rival Oils, Expected Inventory Decline -- Market Talk

1037 GMT - Palm oil closed higher, supported by overnight gains in rival oils and continued buying momentum after breaching the 4,500-ringgit level, Kenanga Futures writes in a note. Expectations for a decline in Malaysia's September inventories for the first time in seven months, and optimism on Indonesia's progress in testing its 50% palm-based biodiesel blend are further lifting market sentiment. However, upside could be limited by potential profit-taking pressure and traders' caution ahead of Malaysia Palm Oil Board data due Friday, Kenanga adds. It sees support for the December futures contract at 4,500 ringgit a ton and resistance at 4,630 ringgit. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for December delivery rose 49 ringgit to 4,594 ringgit a ton. (jason.chau@wsj.com)

--

Teck Resources' Guidance Cut Shows Production Growth Further Away Than Expected -- Market Talk

0946 GMT - Teck Resources' operational update and revised production guidance could stymie calls from its shareholders for an improved offer from Anglo American, Berenberg analysts Richard Hatch and William Dalby write. The two miners have agreed to merge in one of the mining industry's largest ever deals. Teck's guidance cut shows that expected production growth will take longer to achieve than first thought, they write. Anglo American backed Teck's update, saying it is broadly in line with its due diligence and said it has addressed a similar issue to Teck's at Quebrada Blanca, Chile. This is a good start and will help assuage market concerns, they add. Anglo American shares trade up 1.8% at 2,951 pence while Tech's shares closed at 60.6 Canadian dollars Wednesday. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

--

Cocoa Falls on Softening Demand, Improved Weather -- Market Talk

0845 GMT - Cocoa prices fall in early trading on signs of weakening global demand and improved weather conditions in West Africa, marking a significant change in trajectory. Futures in New York are down 0.7% to $5,956 a ton after tumbling to a 20-month low in the previous session. "Cocoa bean processing has declined over the last three quarters in Asia, Europe, and North America, and is widely expected to drop further in the upcoming quarterly reports due next week," analysts at ING say. "Meanwhile, good rains have boosted crop development in the Ivory Coast and Ghana, while both nations have raised farmgate prices this season, encouraging farmers to increase deliveries to warehouses." (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

European Gas Price Falls on Easing Supply Concerns -- Market Talk

0828 GMT - European natural-gas prices fall in early trading, with the benchmark Dutch TTF contract down 1.7% to 32.14 euros a megawatt hour. Storage sites across the EU are at nearly 83% of capacity, according to industry group Gas Infrastructure Europe. "While this is lower than the five-year seasonal average of 91%, it's on schedule to reach its targeted levels before the heating season begins," ANZ analysts say. The region is also benefiting from strong imports of liquefied natural gas, with demand in North Asian markets cooling down due to recent price hikes and high inventories in Japan. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

Copper Gains as Teck Resources Production Guidance Cut Raises Supply Concerns -- Market Talk

0809 GMT - Copper prices climb in early trading after Canadian mining giant Teck Resources cut its production guidance due to continued challenges at its mine in Chile, raising concerns over global supply. Futures on the London Metal Exchange rise 1.6% to $10,874 a metric ton, reaching their highest level since May 2024. "Copper has surged around 23% so far this year, driven by supply disruptions at major mines that outweigh weak demand in major industrial economies," ING analysts say. Meanwhile, the International Copper Study Group forecasts a supply deficit of 150,000 tons next year from previous estimates of a 209,000-ton surplus. The group also narrowed its 2025 surplus estimates to 178,000 tons from 289,000 tons earlier. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

Gold Eases After Gaza Deal But Holds Near Record Highs -- Market Talk

0752 GMT - Gold prices fall in early trading as the first phase of a Gaza deal between Israel and Hamas eases geopolitical risks, but continue to hover near record highs. Futures in New York are down 0.5% to $4,051.40 a troy ounce after hitting an all-time high in the previous session. The precious metal continues to trade above the $4,000 historic mark as investors seek safety amid growing economic and political uncertainty. "Concerns over the U.S. economy and the government shutdown continue to boost investor demand," analysts at ANZ Research say. Strong ETF inflows and expectations that the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates further this year are also supporting prices, with major Wall Street banks saying the yellow metal has room to climb even higher. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

Oil Little Changed as Investors Focus on Gaza Deal -- Market Talk

0737 GMT - Oil prices are little changed in early trading, as investors turn their focus on a long-sought deal in Gaza that could ease geopolitical risks in the Middle East. Brent crude and WTI edge 0.3% higher to $62.19 and $66.43 a barrel, respectively, after opening the session lower. "The immediate impact on the oil markets will be a slight decrease in the geopolitical risk premium--about 1%-2% of the current Brent price--as the markets figure out the details of the peace plan," says Claudio Galimberti, chief economist at Rystad Energy. "In the longer term, if the plan for stable peace proves credible, its impact on prices could be more structural and profound." The repercussions for oil markets could be significant, from diminished disruptions in the Red Sea to renewed prospects for a nuclear deal with Iran and, potentially, a rise in Iranian crude exports, Galimberti says. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

London's Miners Lead FTSE 100 as Attention Turns to Copper -- Market Talk

0736 GMT - London's miners lead the FTSE 100 in opening trade as investor attention turns to copper. Anglo American is the index's largest gainer and trades up 3.1%. Chilean copper miner Antofagasta rises 2.7%, Rio Tinto's London-listed shares are up 2.2% and Glencore rises 1.2%. Copper may be the next hot metals trade, and there is a growing consensus that it might surge to record highs, research director at XTB Kathleen Brooks says. Gold has stolen the limelight this year, but the rise of AI means it's copper's time to shine, she adds. LME copper falls 0.5% but CME copper rises 1.7% and SHFE copper jumps 4.3%.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

--

China's Latest Rare-Earth Controls to Have Limited Impact on Exports -- Market Talk

0451 GMT - China's latest step up in rare-earth controls will likely have limited impact on the actual flow of the material's exports, ING chief China economist Lynn Song says. China introduced new controls on rare-earth exports on Thursday, requiring foreign entities to obtain government approval before re-exporting products of Chinese origin to other countries. Rare earths have been a bargaining chip for China in trade negotiations with the U.S. With a month to go before the truce is set to expire, ING thinks that the two sides could reach another extension. "Closing potential back channels for redirecting rare-earth exports shouldn't materially change these discussions as full-on export controls have always been an available tool to be adjusted as needed," he says. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

--

Iron Ore Rises on Higher Seasonal Demand -- Market Talk

0335 GMT - Iron ore rises in early Asian trade, with the contract for December delivery on the Dalian Commodity Exchange rising 1.0% to 800.5 yuan a ton. The industry remained stable during the market closure for China's Golden Week, with shipments steady and steel mills restocking on higher end-user seasonal demand, analysts at Nanhua Futures write in a note. There is a decline in inventories and a slight improvement in fundamentals, they say. The market will likely be concerned with efforts to limit China's steel exports to the EU, after European officials proposed a 50% tariff on steel imports above a certain quota, the ANZ Research team writes in a note. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

--

Chinese Gold Miners Set to Shine on Higher Gold Prices -- Market Talk

(MORE TO FOLLOW) Dow Jones Newswires

10-09-25 1221ET

data.title: GM relaunches affordable Bolt EV
data.description:
(Reuters) -General Motors said on Thursday it was bringing its affordable Bolt electric vehicle back, as the Detroit automaker looks to widen its offerings in the space.

The Bolt's launch comes at a time when tariff-induced higher prices and fewer EV-related federal subsidies are pressuring consumers.

GM said the Bolt would launch at a starting price of just under $30,000, with a cheaper variant scheduled to be rolled out later in the year.

The EV would be offered with Tesla's North American Charging Standard and is expected to have a range of 255 miles, the company said.

(Reporting by Nathan Gomes in Bengaluru; Editing by Shreya Biswas)

data.title: Meta adds translation support for Hindi and Portuguese in Facebook and Instagram Reels
Citizens Unveils Citizens Edge??
data.description:
Citizens Financial Group, Inc. now have a new way to spend, earn rewards and manage payments with Citizens Edge??, a new commercial credit card from Citizens. Launched in partnership with Mastercard, Citizens Edge offers a robust suite of features ranging from flexible credit limits and cash back rewards to integrated travel and expense management, secure virtual card options and 24/7 client support. Citizens Edge is built to meet the unique needs of middle market companies, a segment of the economy that accounts for one-third of gross domestic product (GDP) and $6 trillion of business-to-business spending in the United States.

These companies are scaling rapidly but often lack access to the sophisticated financial tools available to larger enterprises. Citizens Edge fills that gap with a flexible, secure solution. The launch of Citizens Edge is the latest example of Citizens' deep commitment to delivering innovative payment solutions that help middle market companies scale with confidence.

It is the first commercial credit card introduced through Mastercard's Middle Market Accelerator program, and builds on the momentum of Citizens' recently launched consumer credit card suite, reflecting a broader strategy to deliver modern, flexible solutions across customer segments. As middle market companies seek to grow, digitize and safeguard their businesses, Citizens' 2025 Payment Trends Report and Mastercard research have found that they face challenges with inefficient underwriting and access to digital tools, while fraud is a key concern. Citizens Edge meets these urgent market needs with tailored features including: Flexible credit limits to help scale; Cash back rewards starting at 1.5% that grow as grow; Integrated travel booking and expense management powered by Navan; Security features including a secure virtual card option to enhance protection and mitigate fraud; Personalized, 24/7 customer support for immediate assistance.

In addition, the card offers valuable protections such as extended warranties, purchase assurance and Mastercard ID Theft Protection, providing added peace of mind. Travel perks such as Mastercard Travel Rewards, VAT Reclaim, and Priority Pass airport lounge access help cardholders maximize the value of Citizens Edge wherever business takes them. To simplify card program administration, Citizens has also introduced a new Commercial Card Management page accessible through Citizens' universal commercial client portal, using single sign-on for seamless integration with other banking tools.

The centralized, self-serve digital platform enables companies to manage their accounts, monitor spending and access real-time reporting all in one place.

Mercadolibre to enter Brazil's online medicine market after acquiring first drugstore
US-based Flagship Gold signs deal to revive Mali's Morila mine
Banks weigh on FTSE 100, but Delta Air lifts IAG
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Falls in heavyweight lenders HSBC Holdings and Lloyds Banking Group, plus ex-dividend stocks, saw the FTSE 100 fall from recent record highs on Thursday.

The FTSE 100 index closed down 39.47 points, 0.4%, at 9,509.40. The FTSE 250 ended up 11.00 points, 0.1%, at 22,052.83, and the AIM All-Share fell 2.32 points, 0.3%, to 793.70.

The Cboe UK 100 ended down 0.4% at 950.46, the Cboe UK 250 closed 0.3% higher at 19,307.50, while the Cboe Small Companies declined 0.3% to 17,911.72.

HSBC, the second most valuable firm on London's blue-chip index, gave back 5.4% as analysts questioned the price paid as it bought out the minority stake in Hong Kong-based Hang Seng Bank.

HSBC said it has offered HKD155 per share, around USD19.92, for the 37% of Hang Seng Bank it does not already own.

The offer values Hang Seng Bank at HKD290.31 billion, about USD37.31 billion, and HSBC's holding at HKD106.16 billion, or USD13.62 billion.

Citi banking analyst Andrew Coombs said: "While strategic rationale is compelling, and this seems a sensible overall use of capital, we expect investors will query why now and at this price."

Shore Capital banking analyst Gary Greenwood said the acquisition multiple looks "punchy".

HSBC will also pause share buybacks for three quarters as it rebuilds its CET1 ratio to its target operating range of 14.0% to 14.5%.

The Asian-focused lender estimates an impact on its CETI ratio from the deal to be around 125 basis points.

Meanwhile, Lloyds Banking Group reversed Wednesday's gains, closing down 3.3%, after signalling it may need to make an additional "material" provision relating to the car finance mis-selling.

Lloyds added that "uncertainties remain outstanding on the interpretation and implementation of the proposals" and that the size of the potential provision remains subject to "ongoing analysis and review".

The update comes just two days after the FCA said UK lenders face a combined GBP8.2 billion in compensation payments to customers affected by car finance mis-selling, plus GBP2.8 billion in administrative costs, taking the total cost to around GBP11 billion.

KBW currently forecasts total impairments of around GBP2 billion for Lloyds, compared with the GBP1.15 billion already taken, noting that market share analysis of the FCA's GBP11 billion total "suggests 15% or GBP1.65 billion".

Close Brothers slumped 13% as it too warned the FCA's compensation scheme is likely to result in a material increase in its existing provision of GBP165 million.

Defence stocks also lagged after Israel and Hamas agreed to terms for the release of all hostages held by Hamas in Gaza in a major step toward ending the conflict, with Babcock International down 1.1% and BAE Systems down 0.5%.

"This might simply be a small bout of profit taking after a strong run for the defence sector. Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022 drove more investors to look at defence stocks and a subsequent heightening in geopolitical tensions in other parts of the world led various governments to spend more money on military and intelligence capabilities," commented Russ Mould, investment director, at AJ Bell.

Peace hopes in the Middle East and well received results from US peer Delta Air Lines gave British Airways owner, IAG, a boost, sending it up 3.2% and to the top of the FTSE 100 leaderboard.

The US airline delivered stronger than expected earnings over the last quarter and gave an upbeat outlook.

Advertising agency WPP and housebuilder Barratt Redrow were prominent blue-chip fallers, down 5.9% and 3.6%, as they traded ex-dividend.

Adding to the downward pressure on housebuilders a downbeat survey from the Royal Institution of Chartered Surveyors.

The RICS report showed new buyer enquiries fell for the third month in a row, house prices remain negative and sentiment on near-term outlook remains cautious.

Anthony Codling, analyst at RBC Capital Markets, said things are unlikely to change ahead of the budget in November.

"The sector looks cheap on a medium-to-long term view, but in the shorter term there are fewer reasons to be cheerful," he added.

In European equities on Thursday, the CAC 40 in Paris closed down 0.2% while the DAX 40 in Frankfurt ended up 0.1%.

Shares in Italian car maker Ferrari plunged 14% after its aims for 2030 fell short of loftier forecasts.

Ebitda of at least EUR3.6 billion is expected for 2030. RBC noted this implies a compound annual growth rate of 6%, well below the 10% rise implied in the 2022-2026 forecast outlined three years prior.

"The 6% CAGR is likely conservative, but investors are likely to interpret a downshift in Ebit growth from prior history," RBC analysts commented.

Meanwhile, minutes from the most recent European Central Bank meeting showed all policymakers supported the move to leave rates unchanged.

There was "no immediate pressure to change policy rates at the current meeting" the minutes said.

"The environment remained more uncertain than usual, especially because of the still volatile global trade policy environment but also owing to geopolitical developments. Such uncertainty could also justify keeping interest rates unchanged," the minutes read.

Sterling drifted lower as the dollar gained strength. The pound was quoted lower at USD1.3305 at the time of the London equity market close on Thursday, compared to USD1.3406 on Wednesday.

The euro stood at USD1.1563 compared to USD1.1615. Against the yen, the dollar was trading at JPY153.11, higher compared to JPY152.68.

Stocks in New York were lower at the time of the London close. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, the S&P 500 index and the Nasdaq Composite all traded down 0.3%.

The yield on the US 10-year Treasury was quoted at 4.15%, widened from 4.12% on Wednesday. The yield on the US 30-year Treasury stood at 4.73%, stretched from 4.71%.

Gold traded at USD4,020.10 an ounce on Thursday, down against USD4,044.28 on Wednesday. Brent oil traded at USD65.95 a barrel on Thursday, down from USD66.40 late Wednesday.

The biggest risers on the FTSE 100 were IAG, up 12.80 pence at 412.80p, Anglo American, up 63.00p at 2,963.00p, Weir Group, up 58.00p at 2,860.00p, SSE, up 32.50p at 1,817.00p and Schroders, up 6.20p at 399.00p.

The biggest fallers on the FTSE 100 were WPP, down 21.70p at 345.90p, HSBC, down 57.40p at 1,008.60p, Barratt Redrow, down 14.00p at 375.40p, Lloyds Banking Group, down 2.88p at 83.50p and Burberry Group, down 42.00p at 1,223.00p.

Friday's global economic calendar sees producer price inflation data in Japan and the Michigan consumer sentiment index in the US.

Friday's UK corporate calendar has a trading statement from recruitment company, Hays.

By Jeremy Cutler, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to newsroom@alliancenews.com

Reflection AI, Inc. announced that it has received $2 billion in funding from NVIDIA Corporation
data.description: Reflection AI, Inc. announced that it has received $2 billion in round of funding led by new investor NVIDIA Corporation on October 9, 2025. The company issued Convertible Preferred Stock in the transaction. The company has raised $8 billion as post money valuation.

data.title: EU Ready to Take Action Against Italy's 'Golden Power' in Bank M&A
data.description:
The European Commission is prepared to take action against Italy's "golden power" regulations as part of a broader initiative targeting EU countries that hinder banking consolidation across Europe.

This information was reported by four sources with direct knowledge of the matter.

(Italian version by Andrea Mandalà, editing by Alessia Pé)

Colonial Williamsburg to Receive $330 Million From Bond Sale
data.description:
By Patrick Sheridan

The Economic Development Authority of the City of Williamsburg, Virginia will offer $330 million to finance capital improvements including an arrivals center and additional parking in the historic Colonial Williamsburg district.

The Colonial Williamsburg Foundation Project Series 2025 revenue bonds are federally taxable, but state tax-exempt, and are expected to be priced on Oct. 21, with closing slated for Nov. 4, according to the roadshow document accompanying the preliminary official statement posted Wednesday on MuniOS.

Interest on the bonds is payable starting on May 1 and again on Nov. 1.

The bonds are limited obligations of the authority and the debt is backed solely by payments from the Colonial Williamsburg Foundation under the loan agreement and from other funds available to the trustee under the indenture.

Proceeds will be loaned to the Colonial Williamsburg Foundation and used for the construction of additional parking and an arrivals center, as well as the restoration of existing colonial-era structures and gardens. Monies will also be used to refinance outstanding debt and cover the cost of issuance.

Colonial Williamsburg, is a not-for-profit 501(c)3 educational institution that bills itself as the largest U.S. history museum in the world. The 301-acre campus includes 89 original buildings and more than 500 re-creations of lost structures, as well as two art museums. The Colonial Williamsburg Foundation conducts ongoing historical, architectural, and archaeological research that underpins exhibitions and programs in the Historic Area, in the Art Museums, and online, according to the organization's website.

S&P Global ratings has assigned a ratings of A+ to the bonds.

Wells Fargo Securities is senior manager on the offering.

Write to Patrick Sheridan at patrick.sheridan@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-09-25 1155ET

Bio-Thera Solutions Announces Expansion of Partnership with Intas Pharmaceuticals for BAT2506, A Proposed Biosimilar Referencing Simponi®?, Through Exclusive Commercialization and License Agreement in Canada
data.description:
Bio-Thera Solutions announced an expansion of its partnership with Intas Pharmaceuticals for BAT2506, a proposed golimumab biosimilar, through an exclusive commercialization and license agreement for Canada. BAT2506 is a proposed golimumab bios biosimilar developed by Bio-Thera.olimumab is a human IgG1 monoclonal antibody that targets tumor necrosis factor alpha (TNF-), a pro-inflammatory molecule. Binding of golimumab to TNF- results in reductions in C-reactive protein (CRP), Interleukin 6 (IL-6), Intercellular Adhesion Molecule 1 (ICAM-1), Matrix Metalloproteinase 3 (MMP-3), and Vascular Endothelial Growth Factor (VEGF), all inflammatory markers.

The reference medicine golimumab has been approved in the Canada for several indications, including rheumatoid arthritis, psoriatic arthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, and ulcerative colitis.

data.title: What Carlyle's Proprietary Data Signal About the Economy
data.description:
Jason Thomas, Carlyle's (CG) head of global research and investment strategy, discusses the firm's own estimates of US economic data, which show a grim read on the labor market. The US Labor Department's September employment report was expected by economists in a Bloomberg poll to show a 54,000 increase in nonfarm payrolls. Carlyle estimates that just 17,000 jobs were created. He speaks on "Bloomberg Open Interest."

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires
